concerns.text: Task T011 instructs downloading data from 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/numenta/NAB/master/data/...' (an anomaly detection benchmark) instead of the American Gut Project (AGP) or Open Humans sources mandated by FR-001 and the Plan. This creates a critical data source mismatch; no task exists to implement the actual AGP/Open Humans ingestion required to satisfy FR-001.

Phase 6 (Tasks T037-T042) attempts to implement 'molecular binding affinity estimation' and 'structural interaction models'. The Spec (US-1 to US-3) and Plan explicitly limit the study scope to statistical association (correlation, GLM, bootstrap) and treat molecular data as an unavailable assumption. There is no FR or SC authorizing molecular modeling tasks; these tasks represent unverified scope creep that cannot satisfy any existing requirement.

Task T024 implements GLM with 'diet type' but fails to explicitly document the substitution for 'diet timing' (required by FR-004) in the task description. While the Plan mitigates this, the task itself lacks the traceability note required to prove the deviation from the spec's explicit requirement, leaving FR-004 partially unverified in the implementation plan.

Task T040 phrasing 'study remains at statistical association level' conflates the study's conclusion with its design. The Spec (US-3, FR-008) requires the *report* to frame findings as associational, not the code to enforce a 'limitation' flag. This is a prose issue in the task description that could lead to misinterpretation of the requirement's intent.
